mapping.a_element: mean-field Hamiltonian derived from spin-glass theory
mapping.b_element: thermodynamic description using wave turbulence theory with ultraviolet cut-off

mapping.a_element: phase diagram in terms of pumping rate and degree of disorder
mapping.b_element: phase transition in energy density (thermodynamic limit)
non-condensed phase (high energy density, no large-scale coherence)

mapping.a_element: spin-glass phase (frozen phases in exponentially many configurations)
mapping.b_element: subcritical transition to condensation due to nonlinear interactions
nonlinear interactions modifying the condensation transition

brief_justification: Both papers employ a thermodynamic or statistical mechanics framework to describe phase transitions in nonlinear wave systems. Paper A uses spin-glass theory to derive a phase diagram for mode-locking, while Paper B uses wave turbulence theory to model condensation. The core machinery—phase transitions governed by a mean-field or thermodynamic description—is shared, with analogous phases and critical parameters.
